facts: OpenClaw runs in WSL and primarily operates on D:\openclaw.
Windows paths should be handled via their WSL mappings rather than treated as inaccessible.
Mouse control uses the socket-based windows-bridge flow with host 172.18.160.1.
decisions: Keep long-lived reusable assets in D:\openclaw and keep transient workspace files elsewhere when possible.
Do not configure tray, mouse bridge, or related scripts to auto-start on Windows.
Treat D:\openclaw\selfstart as a manual-start directory, not a startup directory.
Use the fixed Xiaohongshu MCP troubleshooting order before trying extra background checks.
failures: Startup behavior was previously implemented in the wrong direction by treating selfstart and helper scripts as an auto-start chain.
progress: Integrated OpenClaw Memory Sidecar into the real OpenClaw chat path and confirmed runtime sync to D:\openclaw.
anti_patterns: Do not rebuild Windows auto-start chains unless the user explicitly authorizes it.
Do not keep raw logs or cache inventories as long-term memory when a concise conclusion is enough.
